description: Override appliance temporal-worker PBKDF2 values to 12 salt bytes, 1,000 iterations, 64-byte key length, and SHA-512 without changing hosted defaults.
Bundle a production-safe initial-admin password reset script in the Alga core image.
Have the reset script derive hashing through @alga-psa/core/encryption with application-equivalent environment configuration.
Require exactly one internal user matching the appliance tenant ID and original setup-admin email before mutation.
Update only the matched user's hashed_password atomically and fail closed for zero or multiple matches.
Add an authenticated host-service operation for initial Alga admin password recovery.
Derive the target tenant ID and admin email from appliance-owned state rather than browser input.
Validate the new password and confirmation using the existing appliance initial-admin password policy.
Create a uniquely named short-lived Kubernetes Secret carrying the replacement password without placing plaintext in metadata or command arguments.
Create a uniquely named Kubernetes Job using the currently deployed Alga core image and application-equivalent DB, pepper, and PBKDF2 bindings.
Reject a reset request while another initial-admin reset Job is active.
Wait for Job completion and return only generic success or failure information to the browser.
Delete transient Job and Secret resources after completion and configure TTL fallback cleanup.
Extend control-plane RBAC only as required to manage the reset Job and Secret resources.
Add a Reset Alga admin password control to the authenticated appliance status UI.
Display the immutable original admin email without offering a target-user picker.
Provide new-password, confirmation, validation, busy, success, and failure UI states.
Ensure reset inputs and transient credentials are excluded from logs, support output, resource metadata, and API responses.
